metadata.convention: expected_direction compares perturbation to WT baseline (or mutant alone for rescues); increased/decreased/unchanged at direction_threshold 0.05

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Mingli Xu, Tieqiang Hu, Jianfei Zhao, Mee-Yeon Park, Keith W Earley, Gang Wu, Li Yang, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: 35S::MIR156A: LD flowering: 35.4 +/- 0.3 days vs Col 27.8 +/- 0.3 days (+7.6 days delay); juvenile leaves 26.4 +/- 0.5 vs Col 5.0 +/- 0.2 (+21.4 leaves)
evidence.claim: miR156 overexpression significantly delays flowering and prolongs the juvenile phase, increasing plant height

evidence.title: The microRNA regulated SBP-box genes SPL9 and SPL15 control shoot maturation in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Stefan Schwarz, Arne V Grande, Nora Bujdoso, Heinz Saedler, Peter Huijser
evidence.evidence_sentence: Inflorescence height was measured between the rosette and the first flower; double mutant showed significantly reduced height (6.9 cm vs 12.1 cm in wild-type); double mutant produced approximately six extra rosette leaves compared to wild-type (19.5 vs 13.1 leaves); bolted and flowered somewhat later than wild-type
evidence.claim: spl9 spl15 double mutant delays flowering and alters inflorescence architecture

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Mingli Xu, Tieqiang Hu, Jianfei Zhao, Mee-Yeon Park, Keith W Earley, Gang Wu, Li Yang, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: spl2/9/11/13/15 in LD: days to flower 33.0 +/- 0.3 vs Col 27.8 +/- 0.3 (+5.2 days); LFY, AP1, FUL, SOC1 transcripts all reduced approximately 50%
evidence.claim: spl2/9/11/13/15 quintuple mutant delays flowering significantly in long days

evidence.title: The sequential action of miR156 and miR172 regulates developmental timing in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Gang Wu, Mee Yeon Park, Susan R Conway, Jia-Wei Wang, Detlef Weigel, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: pSPL9::rSPL9 accelerated the expression of all adult-specific leaf traits, producing leaves with an elongated leaf blade, serrated leaf margin, and abaxial trichomes; increased miR172 levels >4-fold
evidence.claim: miR156-resistant SPL9 overexpression accelerates adult phase transition and promotes early flowering, decreasing plant height

evidence.title: Regulation of Flowering Time and Floral Organ Identity by a MicroRNA and Its APETALA2-Like Target Genes
evidence.authors: Milo J Aukerman, Hajime Sakai
evidence.evidence_sentence: toe1-2 toe2-1 double mutant: 6.0 rosette leaves vs wild-type 11.4 (significantly earlier than single mutants); The early-flowering phenotype of toe1-2 toe2-1 is consistent with a model in which miR172 causes early flowering by downregulating several AP2-like floral repressors
evidence.claim: toe1 toe2 double mutant causes early flowering and decreased plant height

evidence.title: Regulation of Flowering Time and Floral Organ Identity by a MicroRNA and Its APETALA2-Like Target Genes
evidence.authors: Milo J Aukerman, Hajime Sakai
evidence.evidence_sentence: 35S-EAT (miR172 OE): 2.0 rosette leaves at flowering; EAT-D flowers extremely early with 3.1 rosette leaves vs wild-type 11.4; miR172 overexpression caused early flowering phenotype
evidence.claim: miR172 overexpression causes early flowering and decreased plant height by repressing AP2-like floral repressors

evidence.title: Autoregulation of FCA pre-mRNA processing controls Arabidopsis flowering time
evidence.authors: Victor Quesada, Richard Macknight, Caroline Dean, Gordon G Simpson
evidence.evidence_sentence: late flowering fca-1 mutant plants show delayed floral transition compared to wild-type; FCA functions in parallel to the vernalization pathway to down-regulate RNA levels of the floral repressor FLC
evidence.claim: fca-1 mutant causes late flowering via FLC accumulation, leading to increased plant height

evidence.title: FPA, a Gene Involved in Floral Induction in Arabidopsis, Encodes a Protein Containing RNA-Recognition Motifs
evidence.authors: Fritz M Schomburg, David A Patton, David W Meinke, Richard M Amasino
evidence.evidence_sentence: fpa-2 mutant: 17.8 +/- 1.4 rosette leaves in long days vs wild-type 7.2 +/- 0.6; mutations in FPA result in extremely delayed flowering
evidence.claim: fpa mutant causes late flowering via FLC accumulation, increasing plant height

evidence.title: Research progress on the autonomous flowering time pathway in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Jing-Zhi Cheng, Yu-Ping Zhou, Tian-Xiao Lv, Chu-Ping Xie, Chang-En Tian
evidence.journal: Physiology and Molecular Biology of Plants
evidence.evidence_sentence: FLD contains an amine oxidase domain and is a homolog of the human H3K4me demethylase LSD1; FLD demethylates H3K4me2 at the FLC gene body; most mutants for above genes are recessive and showed late-flowering phenotype under both long- and short-day conditions
evidence.claim: fld mutant causes late flowering via FLC accumulation, leading to increased plant height

evidence.title: FVE, an Arabidopsis Homologue of the Retinoblastoma-Associated Protein That Regulates Flowering Time and Cold Response, Binds to Chromatin as a Large Multiprotein Complex
evidence.authors: Jin Jeon, Jungmook Kim
evidence.evidence_sentence: In the fve mutants, the FLC chromatin was enriched in acetylated histones H3 and H4, indicating FVE is required for histone deacetylation at FLC chromatin; late-flowering phenotype of acg1 was complemented by FVE overexpression
evidence.claim: fve mutant causes FLC accumulation and late flowering via failure of histone deacetylation at FLC, increasing plant height

evidence.title: Hypomorphic Alleles Reveal FCA-Independent Roles for FY in the Regulation of FLOWERING LOCUS C
evidence.authors: Wei Feng, Yannick Jacob, Kira M Veley, Lei Ding, Xuhong Yu, Goh Choe, Scott D Michaels
evidence.evidence_sentence: fy-5 caused a 90% increase in FLC transcript and delayed flowering
evidence.claim: fy-5 causes FLC accumulation and late flowering, increasing plant height

evidence.title: Regulation of Flowering Time and Floral Organ Identity by a MicroRNA and Its APETALA2-Like Target Genes
evidence.authors: Milo J Aukerman, Hajime Sakai
evidence.evidence_sentence: Transgenic 35S-TOE1 plants flowered much later than wild-type plants with average 28.6 rosette leaves; toe1-1D activation-tagged line showed late flowering with 22.5 rosette leaves vs 11.4 wild-type
evidence.claim: TOE1 overexpression causes late flowering and increased plant height

evidence.title: Repression of Flowering by the miR172 Target SMZ
evidence.authors: Johannes Mathieu, Levi J Yant, Felix Mürdter, Frank Küttner, Markus Schmid
evidence.evidence_sentence: smz-D (LD): 45.1 +/- 1.7 leaves vs WT 15.5 +/- 0.6; 35S::rSMZ plants remained vegetative throughout their life
evidence.claim: SMZ overexpression causes severe late flowering and increased plant height

evidence.title: Repression of Flowering by the miR172 Target SMZ
evidence.authors: Johannes Mathieu, Levi J Yant, Felix Mürdter, Frank Küttner, Markus Schmid
evidence.evidence_sentence: toe1 toe2 smz snz quadruple mutant: 7.7 +/- 0.7 leaves, flowers significantly earlier (p<0.001), confirming redundant repression of flowering by AP2-like genes
evidence.claim: Loss of TOE1, TOE2, SMZ (and SNZ) causes early flowering and decreased plant height

evidence.title: Sugar promotes vegetative phase change in Arabidopsis thaliana by repressing the expression of MIR156A and MIR156C
evidence.authors: Li Yang, Mingli Xu, Yeonjong Koo, Jia He, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: Exogenous sucrose significantly accelerates abaxial trichome production in wild-type plants; sugar treatment reduced pri-MIR156A and pri-MIR156C abundance and mature miR156 levels, while SPL9 and SPL15 transcripts were markedly increased
evidence.claim: Sucrose treatment represses miR156, promotes adult phase transition, accelerates flowering, and decreases plant height

evidence.title: Arabidopsis Relatives of the Human Lysine-Specific Demethylase1 Repress the Expression of FWA and FLOWERING LOCUS C and Thus Promote the Floral Transition
evidence.authors: Danhua Jiang, Wannian Yang, Yuehui He, Richard M Amasino
evidence.evidence_sentence: ldl1 mutants flowered later than wild-type Columbia (Col) in long days; FLC was upregulated in ldl1 and ldl1 ldl2 mutants
evidence.claim: ldl1 mutant causes late flowering via FLC accumulation, increasing plant height

evidence.title: Arabidopsis Relatives of the Human Lysine-Specific Demethylase1 Repress the Expression of FWA and FLOWERING LOCUS C and Thus Promote the Floral Transition
evidence.authors: Danhua Jiang, Wannian Yang, Yuehui He, Richard M Amasino
evidence.evidence_sentence: ldl1-2 ldl2 double mutant flowered later than ldl1 single mutant; FLD, LDL1, and LDL2 act in partial redundancy to repress FLC expression
evidence.claim: ldl1 ldl2 double mutant causes enhanced late flowering via FLC accumulation, increasing plant height

evidence.title: Autoregulation of FCA pre-mRNA processing controls Arabidopsis flowering time
evidence.authors: Victor Quesada, Richard Macknight, Caroline Dean, Gordon G Simpson
evidence.evidence_sentence: The presence of the FCA-gamma transgene overcomes FRI epistasis and plants flower earlier (21.8 +/- 0.3 leaves in FRI control vs 13.5 +/- 0.3 leaves with transgene)
evidence.claim: FCA overexpression causes early flowering by repressing FLC, reducing plant height

evidence.title: Systematic analyses of the MIR172 family members of Arabidopsis define their distinct roles in regulation of APETALA2 during floral transition
evidence.authors: Ó'Maoiléidigh DS, van Driel AD, Singh A, Sang Q
evidence.evidence_sentence: mir172abcde plants flowered much later than Col-0; mir172abd and mir172abcde plants initially flowering from axillary shoots when grown under short photoperiods (approximately 91% of 77 plants); FT mRNA levels were between 27% and 31% of Col-0 levels in mir172abcde quintuple mutants
evidence.claim: Loss of miR172 causes late flowering via accumulation of AP2-like repressors, increasing plant height

evidence.title: Developmental Functions of miR156-Regulated SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) Genes in Arabidopsis thaliana
evidence.authors: Mingli Xu, Tieqiang Hu, Jianfei Zhao, Mee-Yeon Park, Keith W Earley, Gang Wu, Li Yang, R Scott Poethig
evidence.evidence_sentence: spl9/13/15 in SD: 31.5 +/- 0.8 juvenile leaves vs Col 7.0 +/- 0.2; 59.8 +/- 1.1 rosette leaves vs Col 42.7 +/- 0.7; spl9/13/15 produced 16-24 more juvenile leaves and 6-17 more rosette leaves than Col
evidence.claim: spl9/13/15 triple mutant significantly extends the juvenile phase and increases rosette leaf number in short days

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis

evidence.authors: Sun TP

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP

evidence.title: Gibberellin Metabolism, Perception and Signaling Pathways in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Sun TP
